member.office_address: 261 Russell Senate Office Building Washington DC 20510

form_analysis.form_header_text: Senator Bennet FY27 Programmatic Appropriations Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: The Office of Senator Bennet will begin accepting FY27 Appropriations Requests on Wednesday, February 25, 2026. This form will CLOSE on Friday, March 27, 2026 at 5pm MT. Programmatic Appropriations Requests are general funding requests, bill language, or report language requests for national and regional programs under the jurisdiction of a federal agency (i.e. Department of Defense, Department of Energy, Department of Agriculture, etc.). Please note, we will not accept additional requests once this form closes. If you have multiple requests, you will need to resubmit this form for each request.

sections.description: Information about the requesting organization and its primary point of contact.
General categorization and prioritization of the appropriations request.

fields.label: Which subcommittee are you submitting a request for?
fields.rationale: NASA funding falls under the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies subcommittee according to the provided reference values.

fields.label: Priority (enter "1" for first priority, this section is only necessary if you're submitting multiple requests)
fields.rationale: This is a primary programmatic request for a major NASA directorate. Set to 1 assuming this is the submitter's top priority for NASA funding.

fields.label: Type of request (language or funding)
options: Funding (If you are ONLY requesting funding, you do not need to submit a report language request along with a funding request.)
fields.draft_value: Funding (If you are ONLY requesting funding, you do not need to submit a report language request along with a funding request.)
fields.rationale: The request context identifies this as a programmatic funding request for $9,000,000,000 for NASA's Science Mission Directorate.

sections.description: Specific information required for programmatic funding requests.

fields.label: Agency
fields.rationale: Standard agency name as provided in reference values.

fields.label: Account
fields.rationale: Standard account name as provided in reference values.

fields.label: Program Title (Please note that the "Program Title" should not be the same as the Account or Agency Name)
fields.rationale: This is the specific directorate within the Science account being funded.

fields.label: Program Element Number (PE# - Defense Only)
fields.rationale: This is a non-defense programmatic request for NASA Science; Program Element Numbers are specific to Department of Defense accounts.

fields.label: FY26 Funded Amount
fields.help_text: Enter the numeric dollar amount funded in the prior fiscal year.
fields.rationale: FY2026 enacted amount from authoritative funding history.

fields.label: FY27 Request Amount
fields.help_text: Enter the numeric dollar amount requested for FY27.
fields.rationale: The target funding ask for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Please provide a justification for your request. [This should be a one paragraph explanation for why you want the committee to fund this request. This justification will be sent to the Appropriations Committee.]
fields.draft_value: A $9 billion investment in NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) is essential to maintain U.S. leadership in space and drive domestic innovation. This funding supports critical missions like the Lucy and MAVEN spacecraft, both of which utilize the world-class expertise of Colorado's aerospace industry. In Colorado alone, NASA SMD has obligated over $717 million in recent years to local leaders like Lockheed Martin, BAE Systems, and the University of Colorado Boulder. These investments support thousands of high-skilled jobs and ensure the U.S. remains the global partner of choice for space exploration. Sustained funding is necessary to counter rapid international competition and deliver the scientific breakthroughs that define our national prestige and technical edge.
fields.rationale: Combines the $9B ask with specific Colorado spending data (Lockheed, BAE, CU Boulder) and strategic arguments regarding leadership and competition.

fields.label: Was this request included in the President's FY27 Budget Request? (if so please provide the title, amount, and page number)
fields.draft_value: The President's FY2027 Budget Request has not been released at this time.
fields.rationale: The reference values state the FY2027 PBR status is currently unknown.

fields.label: Please explain the Colorado connection.
fields.draft_value:
* Over $717M in NASA Science obligations (FY2023-FY2025) across Colorado aerospace firms and research institutions
* Lockheed Martin (Littleton) supports mission-critical hardware for Lucy, OSIRIS-REx, and MAVEN
* Bae Systems (Boulder) develops main optical assemblies for WFIRST and Earth-monitoring hardware
* CU Boulder and UCAR manage $46M+ in grants for atmospheric science and deep space lunar robotics
fields.rationale: Directly cites local spending totals, major contract recipients (Lockheed Martin, Bae Systems), and leading grant recipients (CU Boulder, UCAR) from provided Colorado spending context.

fields.label: Are you submitting this request to other offices?
fields.rationale: This request is being submitted to multiple offices.

fields.label: Have you worked with the Senate Appropriations Committee on this request?
fields.rationale: The provided context does not specify whether direct coordination with the Senate Appropriations Committee staff has occurred.

fields.label: Please provide the contact information for the committee staffer(s) you worked with on this request.
fields.rationale: This requires specific knowledge of the submitter's personal interactions with congressional staff.

fields.label: Please submit any explanatory, budget, or project related documents here.
fields.rationale: File must be attached manually.

sections.description: Specific information required for bill or report language requests.

fields.label: Language Type
fields.rationale: This field is likely only required if 'Language' was selected as the request type. Since this is a funding request, this section is non-applicable.

fields.label: Agency
fields.rationale: Duplicate field; providing consistent agency name.

fields.label: Account
fields.rationale: Duplicate field; providing consistent account name.

fields.label: Program Title (Please note that the "Program Title" should not be the same as the Account or Agency Name)
fields.rationale: Duplicate field; providing consistent program title.

fields.label: Proposed Language
fields.rationale: The provided request context indicates that no specific report or bill language is being requested for this programmatic funding ask.

fields.label: Please provide a justification for your request. [This should be a one paragraph explanation for why you want the committee to fund this request. This justification will be sent to the Appropriations Committee.]
fields.draft_value:
* $9B for NASA Science maintains U.S. global leadership and competitive edge against China's expanding space ambitions
* Sustains a 3-to-1 economic return on investment and provides workforce stability after FY2026 funding disruptions
* Funds peer-reviewed Decadal Survey priorities that ensure the nation's most impactful scientific questions are addressed
* Bolsters the STEM talent pipeline by supporting high-profile missions to Mars, the Moon, and beyond
fields.rationale: Integrates strategic talking points on international competition, economic ROI (3:1), workforce stability, and the Decadal Survey process.

fields.label: Please explain the Colorado connection.
fields.draft_value:
* Over $717M in NASA Science obligations (FY2023-FY2025) across Colorado aerospace firms and research institutions
* Lockheed Martin (Littleton) supports mission-critical hardware for Lucy, OSIRIS-REx, and MAVEN
* Bae Systems (Boulder) develops main optical assemblies for WFIRST and Earth-monitoring hardware
* CU Boulder and UCAR manage $46M+ in grants for atmospheric science and deep space lunar robotics
fields.rationale: Identical to the previous Colorado connection field; utilizes verified spending data for Lockheed Martin, Bae Systems, CU Boulder, and UCAR.

fields.label: Are you submitting this request to other offices? Please list which offices.
fields.draft_value:
* Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request.
* Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: Have you worked with the Senate Appropriations Committee on this request?
fields.rationale: This is a duplicate of a previous field and its applicability depends on the selected request type. The context does not provide information regarding staff-level engagement.

fields.label: Please provide the contact information for the committee staffer(s) you worked with on this request.
fields.rationale: This requires specific knowledge of the submitter's personal interactions with congressional staff.

fields.label: Please upload a PDF copy of your Report language and a separate PDF providing additional information about your request.
fields.rationale: File must be attached manually.

sections.description: Supplemental fiscal information regarding the request.

fields.label: Most Recent Enacted Appropriations Amount
fields.rationale: Based on the FY2026 enacted figure provided.

fields.label: Previously or currently included in PBR? Please list the amount below
fields.rationale: Provides the FY2026 President's Budget Request amount as the most recent known PBR figure for comparison.

top_contracts.recipient_name: Bae Systems Space & Mission Systems Inc.
top_contracts.description: Engineering services and development leading to the delivery of the omps protoflight model and support.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Developing critical hardware for tracking Earth's atmospheric ozone layer.

top_contracts.description: Origins spectral interpretation resource identification security-regolith explorer (OSIRIS-REx) phase b effort.  Under this contract, the contractor shall provide the resources necessary to complete Mission Design and requirements flow down activities culminating in an OSIRIS-REx project Mission Design Review (MDR). The contractor shall provide the resources necessary to support the OSIRIS-REx project to define and complete the definition of the preliminary Design, and the plans for development, manufacture, test, verification and operation of a flight system.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Establishing the final design for the OSIRIS-REx asteroid explorer.

top_contracts.description: The lucy spacecraft and mission operations contract embodies the mission'S requirement to design, development, test, and operate the lucy spacecraft bus. Lucy will explore six Jupiter trojan asteroids trapped by jupiters gravity in two swarms that share the planets orbit, one leading and one trailing Jupiter in its 12-year circuit around the sun. The trojans are thought to be relics of a much earlier era in the history of the solar system, and may have formed far beyond jupiters current orbit.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Investigating ancient solar system relics via the Lucy mission.

top_contracts.recipient_name: Bae Systems Space & Mission Systems Inc.
top_contracts.description: The woma requirement was issued as a full and open competitive procurement to design, analyze, develop, fabricate, integrate, test, and evaluate the Wide Field Instrument (WFI) opto-mechanical Assembly (WOMA) for the Wide Field infrared survey telescope (WFIRST) Mission. In addition, it defines the tasks necessary to Support the subsequent integration, test, evaluation, and validation of the Wfi. It defines the post-delivery Support to payload and observatory integration and test, and to pre-launch, launch and commissioning activities at the Mission Operations Center (MOC) and to supply and maintain the Instrument Ground Support Equipment (GSE).
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Designing and building the main optical assembly for the WFIRST telescope.

top_contracts.description: Risk reduction and phase b effort in support of the Mars atmospheric and volatile evoluation (MAVEN) project.  The contractor shall provide the resources necessary to support the MAVEN project to define and complete the definition of the preliminary design, and the plans for development, manufacture, test, verification and operation of a flight system, which consists of the integrated spacecraft and science payloads in accordance with the statement of work.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Designing and planning the MAVEN spacecraft to track Mars's atmospheric loss.

top_contracts.recipient_name: Bae Systems Space & Mission Systems Inc.
top_contracts.description: Igf::CL::igf imaging x-ray polarimetry explorer (IXPE) phase a
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Developing the IXPE mission to map cosmic X-ray sources.

top_grants.recipient_name: The Regents of the University of Colorado
top_grants.description: Objectives: the dynamics atmosphere global-connection (DYNAGLO) mission will be the first of its kind  providing the community with global thermosphere gw measurements and their characteristics to be correlated with known gw sources (terrestrial and
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Mapping global atmospheric waves to understand energy sources.

top_grants.description: This proposal outlines continuing collaborations between ucar and NASA for work in support of NASA heliophysics living with a star infrastructure. Cpaess will continue best practices learned over the past five years (2016-2021) for program management
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Program management supporting NASA's "Living with a Star" efforts.

top_grants.description: 23-a3di23_2-0063  leaf beta: lunar effects on agricultural flora
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Investigating Moon's impact on future food crops.

top_grants.recipient_name: The Regents of the University of Colorado
top_grants.description: Multiple year award with incremental funding for years one to five.  This proposal is entitled  "network for exploration and space science  ness." this is awarded in response to cooperative agreement notice  can no. nnh16zda009c  NASA solar system exploration research virtual institute  sservi  cycle three.  The principal investigator is Dr. Jack o. Burns with the university of colorado  uco.  Project summary:  ness will implement cross-disciplinary partnerships to advance scientific discovery and human exploration of target destinations  and will conduct research in astrophysics and heliophysics that is uniquely enabled by human and robotic exploration of target bodies.   Our research is derived from three decadal surveys and from the NASA astrophysics and global exploration roadmaps.  Ness research includes: telerobotic operation of surface science  exploration assets at the moon and Mars  the nature of the moon'S core  an improved selenodetic coordinate system precision tests of gravitation  energetic solar particle events in interplanetary space and extrasolar systems  dust environments of space and target bodies  detection of the universe'S first galaxies  and habitable exoplanets.  Four key projects will be proposed.    Exploration science  ness will mature a critical technology of science and exploration missions from the orbits of target bodies:  surface telerobotics.  We will develop human-robotic science associated with Orion/habitat missions in cis-lunar orbits.  Our team will build tools for the crew to teleoperate rovers on the lunar farside that will feed-forward to the first crewed Mars missions.  We will investigate the systems needed on Orion/habitat to support telerobotics  analyze lunar low frequency radio telescope deployment along with a lunar sample return  and develop crew training strategies.  Llr  current alternate theories of gravity predict deviations from general relativity  gr  at a level that is within the grasp of the next generation of lunar laser ranging  llr  instruments.  Ness will improve by factors greater than 100 the signal photoelectron accuracy of llr to set new constraints on gr and the lunar core.  We will also measure/model dust on llr optical surfaces and significantly improve the selenodetic coordinate system for exploration and science missions.  Heliophysics and space physics_ness heliophysics research directly impacts human exploration via our studies of solar particle radiation and interplanetary dust.  We will advance the design for a lunar-based low frequency  less than 10 mhz  radio imager  uniquely capable of imaging the trajectory of particle acceleration in solar eruptions  and measuring interplanetary nanodust.  From cosmic dawn to habitable planets  ness addresses key questions from the astrophysics decadal survey: "when and how did the first galaxies form?" and "can we find another planet like Earth orbiting a nearby star?" our team will use new modeling tools and technologies to investigate the formation of the first galaxies using the neutral hydrogen 21-cm signal  red-shifted to 2-3 m.  The moon'S farside is uniquely shielded from radio frequency interference and free from ionospheric effects to enable such observations.  A farside observatory is important for exploration to investigate the environmental and technical challenges to deploying/operating a large-area scientific station on the moon.  We will advance 21-cm cosmology theory  the design of a low radio frequency array  wide-band receivers  and new calibration techniques.  Ness also will advance the study of the characteristics of the habitable exoplanets by employing polarimetry on large uv_optical telescopes constructed in cis-lunar space  and by using a farside radio array to detect magnetospheres for earth-like planets.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing lunar surface robotics for deep space science missions.

top_grants.description: This proposal outlines plans that continue collaborations with NASA goddard space flight Center through the Joint Center for Satellite Data Assimilation (JCSDA) in support of NASA missions and associated science. Jcsda will work with NASA to provide
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Continuing JCSDA collaboration for NASA mission data assimilation.

top_grants.recipient_name: Bae Systems Space & Mission Systems Inc.
top_grants.description: The roses funding opportunity goals are: execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress. Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond. Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Funding broad science supporting Moon, Mars, Earth applications, and space weather.

top_grants.description: Windcube: a cubesat thermospheric wind instrument
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing the Windcube instrument for measuring thermospheric winds.
